name: Sardinia (Sardegna / Sardigna)
summary: Sardinia (Sardegna in Italian / Sardigna in Sardinian / officially Regione Autonoma della Sardegna) is a Mediterranean civil-law autonomous region of the Italian Republic comprising the island of Sardinia (second-largest island in the Mediterranean, ~24,090 km²) and surrounding minor archipelagos (La Maddalena, Asinara, San Pietro, Sant'Antioco) — structurally distinctive globally as the only Italian autonomous region operating a constitutionally-protected multi-linguistic minority framework with Sardinian (Sardu), Algherese Catalan (Alguerès), Sassarese (Sassarese), Gallurese (Gadduresu), Tabarchino, and Maddalenino as recognised minority languages under Italian Law 482/1999 and Sardinia's Sardinian Linguistic Policy Law 22/2018, as the second Italian autonomous region established (Statuto Speciale 26 February 1948, ~9 months after Sicily but ~2 months before formal Italian Constitution), and as the central jurisdiction of Italy's longest-tradition continuous customary-law-influenced civil-law framework dating from the medieval Carta de Logu (~14th-century Sardinian customary-law code by Eleonora of Arborea, 1392 Logudoro version). Sardinia's autonomy framework was established by Constitutional Law 3/1948 of 26 February 1948 (Statuto Speciale della Regione Sardegna). The Statuto Speciale grants Sardinia exclusive legislative competence over an extensive range of matters including agriculture, fisheries, industry, mining, and local administration. Family-law framework operates under the Italian Civil Code (Codice Civile 1942, as amended by Law 54/2006 Joint Custody Reform) applied via Sardinian autonomous-region administrative framework with Italian Law 482/1999 linguistic-minority protections. Parental authority (responsabilità genitoriale) and child custody operate under Italian Civil Code Articles 315-342-bis as amended. The Court of Appeal of Cagliari (or Sassari) is the apex regional appellate court for Sardinian civil and criminal matters; final appellate jurisdiction lies with the Italian Court of Cassation and the Italian Constitutional Court. Sardinia is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label. Italy is a Hague Convention 1980 party (acceded 22 February 1985) — Sardinia Hague applicability via Italian territorial extension.

statutory_framework.citation: Statuto Speciale della Regione Sardegna 1948 (Constitutional Law 3/1948)
statutory_framework.title: Sardinian Special Statute
statutory_framework.relevance: Italian Constitutional Law of 26 February 1948 establishing the Sardinian autonomous-region framework — second Italian autonomous region (after Sicily 1946).

statutory_framework.title: Italian Linguistic Minorities Protection Law
statutory_framework.relevance: Italian Law of 15 December 1999 establishing protection framework for historical linguistic minorities — recognises Sardinian, Catalan (Alguerese), and other Sardinian minority languages.

statutory_framework.title: Sardinian Linguistic Policy Law
statutory_framework.relevance: Sardinian Regional Law 22/2018 of 3 July 2018 establishing Sardinia's distinctive multi-linguistic-minority policy framework including Sardinian (Sardu), Algherese Catalan (Alguerès), Sassarese, Gallurese, Tabarchino, and Maddalenino.

statutory_framework.citation: Italian Codice Civile Articles 315-342-bis (as amended by Law 54/2006)
statutory_framework.title: Italian Civil Code — Parental Authority
statutory_framework.relevance: Italian Civil Code articles governing responsabilità genitoriale and child custody applicable in Sardinia.

statutory_framework.citation: Carta de Logu (Sardinian Medieval Customary-Law Code)
statutory_framework.title: Carta de Logu
statutory_framework.relevance: Medieval Sardinian customary-law code by Eleonora of Arborea — 1392 Logudoro version. Foundational influence on Italy's longest-tradition continuous customary-law-influenced civil-law framework.

apex_courts.name: Court of Appeal of Cagliari (Corte d'Appello di Cagliari)
apex_courts.role: Apex regional appellate court for civil and criminal matters from southern Sardinia.

apex_courts.name: Court of Appeal of Sassari (Corte d'Appello di Sassari)
apex_courts.role: Apex regional appellate court for civil and criminal matters from northern Sardinia.

apex_courts.name: Corte di Cassazione (Italian Court of Cassation)
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Sardinia.

professional_regulators.name: Ordine degli Psicologi della Regione Sardegna
professional_regulators.role: Sardinian regional professional regulatory framework for psychology.

anonymisation_convention: Sardinian family-court decisions are anonymised per Italian court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Treaty of Anagni + Aragonese-Crown-Kingdom-of-Sardinia framework
key_developments.description: Treaty of Anagni 24 June 1297 between Pope Boniface VIII and James II of Aragon establishing Kingdom of Sardinia and Corsica as Aragonese-Crown fiefdom — substantively distinctive Mediterranean Aragonese-Crown-Kingdom-of-Sardinia framework. Foundational pre-1720-Savoyard framework affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory and Catalan-language-enclave at Alghero (Algherese Catalan).

key_developments.title: Carta de Logu + Eleonora-of-Arborea + Sardinian-medieval-customary-law-code framework
key_developments.description: Carta de Logu medieval Sardinian customary-law code by Eleonora of Arborea (1392 Logudoro version) — substantively distinctive globally only-medieval-customary-law-code-by-a-female-ruler-in-medieval-Mediterranean framework. Foundational influence on Italy's longest-tradition continuous customary-law-influenced civil-law framework persisting through 21st century affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Treaty of The Hague + Savoyard-Kingdom-of-Sardinia framework
key_developments.description: Treaty of The Hague 17 February 1720 transferring Sardinia from Austria to Savoyard House — substantively distinctive Mediterranean establishing Savoyard Kingdom of Sardinia (1720-1861) framework. Foundational pre-1861-Italian-Unification framework affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory. Subsequent Kingdom-of-Sardinia-as-pre-Italian-state framework affecting Italian Unification.

key_developments.title: Editto delle Chiudende + transition-from-medieval-customary-land-rights framework
key_developments.description: Editto delle Chiudende (Edict of Enclosures) 6 October 1820 by Charles Felix of Sardinia ending medieval-customary-land-rights — substantively distinctive Mediterranean transition-from-medieval-customary-rights-+-Sardinian-rural-revolts framework. Substantive demographic and family-law-implementation disruption affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory and Sardinian-distinctive-rural framework.

key_developments.title: Italian Unification + Sardinia-as-pre-Italian-state framework
key_developments.description: Italian Unification 17 March 1861 with Kingdom of Sardinia transformed into Kingdom of Italy under Savoyard House — substantively distinctive Mediterranean Sardinia-as-pre-Italian-state-+-Sardinian-Savoyard-monarchy-becoming-Italian-monarchy framework. Foundational pre-1948-Statuto-Speciale framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Statuto Speciale + second-Italian-autonomous-region framework
key_developments.description: Italian Constitutional Law 3/1948 of 26 February 1948 establishing the Sardinian autonomous-region framework — substantively distinctive Mediterranean second-Italian-autonomous-region-after-Sicily-1946-and-2-months-before-Italian-Constitution framework. Foundational substantive Sardinian-autonomy framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Italian Hague Convention 1980 accession + Sardinia-applicability framework
key_developments.description: Italy acceded to Hague Convention 1980 effective 22 February 1985 with applicability to Sardinia via Italian territorial extension — substantively significant Mediterranean Hague-applicability framework. Foundational substantive cross-border-jurisdiction-practice framework affecting subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Italian Law 482/1999 + Linguistic-Minorities-Protection-Sardinian-languages framework
key_developments.description: Italian Law 482/1999 of 15 December 1999 establishing protection framework for historical linguistic minorities — substantively distinctive Mediterranean Italian-+-Sardinian-Algherese-Catalan-Sassarese-Gallurese-Tabarchino-Maddalenino-linguistic-minorities framework. Foundational substantive linguistic-minorities framework persisting through subsequent 2018 Sardinian Linguistic Policy Law 22/2018 reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Sardinian Linguistic Policy Law 22/2018 + most-comprehensive-EU-sub-national-linguistic-policy framework
key_developments.description: Sardinian Regional Law 22/2018 of 3 July 2018 establishing Sardinia's distinctive multi-linguistic-minority policy framework — substantively distinctive globally only-Italian-autonomous-region-operating-constitutionally-protected-multi-linguistic-minority-framework-with-six-recognised-minority-languages framework (Sardinian Sardu + Algherese Catalan Alguerès + Sassarese + Gallurese Gadduresu + Tabarchino + Maddalenino). Substantive linguistic-minorities framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Christian Solinas Lega-PSD'Az + Consiglio-Regionale + Difensore-Civico + ZES-Zone-Economica-Speciale framework
key_developments.description: Christian Solinas (Partito Sardo d'Azione-Lega) President framework continuing through 2023 with Consiglio Regionale della Sardegna institutional positions addressing PA-related issues within Italian Codice Civile arts. 315-342-bis + Statuto Speciale 1948 framework applied via Corte d'Appello di Cagliari + Corte d'Appello di Sassari. Difensore Civico Regionale institutional positions affecting cross-border family-law adjudication. Continuing Sardinian ZES (Zona Economica Speciale) framework substantively significant institutional context. Substantively significant Mediterranean Italian-autonomous-region institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Corte d'Appello di Cagliari + Corte d'Appello di Sassari + Corte di Cassazione — responsabilità-genitoriale substantive register + continuing-multi-linguistic-minority framework
key_developments.description: Courts of Appeal of Cagliari and Sassari, and Italian Court of Cassation continue to develop responsabilità genitoriale jurisprudence under Italian Codice Civile articles 315-342-bis + Statuto Speciale 1948 + Italian Law 482/1999 + Sardinian Linguistic Policy Law 22/2018 + Hague Convention 1980 framework in custody disputes within continuing Sardinian autonomous-region + multi-linguistic-minority framework. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Sardinia operates a civil-law Italian Codice Civile framework via Sardinian autonomous-region administrative framework with multi-linguistic-minority protections — places Sardinia in the Mediterranean Italian autonomous-region cluster.

Only Italian autonomous region operating constitutionally-protected multi-linguistic minority framework with six recognised minority languages is structurally distinctive globally — Sardinian, Algherese Catalan, Sassarese, Gallurese, Tabarchino, Maddalenino.
Second Italian autonomous region established (Statuto Speciale 26 February 1948) is structurally distinctive within Italian autonomous-region cluster.
Central jurisdiction of Italy's longest-tradition continuous customary-law-influenced civil-law framework dating from medieval Carta de Logu (1392) is structurally distinctive globally.
Second-largest island in the Mediterranean is structurally distinctive globally.
Sardinian Linguistic Policy Law 22/2018 is structurally distinctive globally — one of the most comprehensive sub-national linguistic-minority policy frameworks in Europe.
Italian Law 482/1999 linguistic-minority protection framework applicability in Sardinia is structurally distinctive within Italian linguistic-minority cluster.
Italian Hague Convention 1980 accession 1985 + Sardinian Italian-civil-law framework intersection is structurally distinctive.
editorial_notes: Sardinia jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 5 to 10 key_developments with full Treaty-of-Anagni-1297-to-continuing-multi-linguistic-minority trajectory: 1297-Treaty-of-Anagni-+-Aragonese-Crown + 1392-Carta-de-Logu-+-Eleonora-of-Arborea + 1720-Treaty-of-The-Hague-+-Savoyard-Kingdom + 1820-Editto-delle-Chiudende + 1861-Italian-Unification + 1948-Statuto-Speciale-second-Italian-autonomous-region + 1985-Italian-Hague-Convention-accession + 1999-Italian-Law-482/1999-Linguistic-Minorities + 2018-Sardinian-Linguistic-Policy-Law-22/2018 + 2024-Corte-d'Appello-+-Corte-di-Cassazione-responsabilità.

Civil-law Mediterranean Italian autonomous region with constitutionally-protected multi-linguistic-minority framework (Italian Codice Civile Articles 315-342-bis as amended by Law 54/2006 + Statuto Speciale della Regione Sardegna 1948 + Italian Law 482/1999 Linguistic Minorities Protection + Sardinian Linguistic Policy Law 22/2018 + Carta de Logu 1392 medieval customary-law foundation + Italian Hague Convention 1980 accession 1985). Only Italian autonomous region operating constitutionally-protected multi-linguistic minority framework with six recognised minority languages globally + second Italian autonomous region + central jurisdiction of Italy's longest-tradition continuous customary-law-influenced civil-law framework + second-largest Mediterranean island + Algherese Catalan Catalan-language enclave on Sardinia + only medieval customary-law code by a female ruler in medieval Mediterranean (Eleonora of Arborea 1392).

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive responsabilità genitoriale analysis under Italian Codice Civile articles 315-342-bis + Statuto Speciale 1948 + Italian Law 482/1999 + Sardinian Linguistic Policy Law 22/2018 + Hague Convention 1980 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Mediterranean + civil-law + Italian-autonomous-region-cluster + multi-linguistic-minority-framework-six-recognised-languages-globally-distinctive-Sardinian-+-Algherese-Catalan-+-Sassarese-+-Gallurese-+-Tabarchino-+-Maddalenino + Sardinian-Linguistic-Policy-Law-22/2018-most-comprehensive-EU-sub-national-linguistic-policy + Carta-de-Logu-1392-Eleonora-of-Arborea-+-only-medieval-customary-law-code-by-female-ruler-globally-distinctive + Algherese-Catalan-Catalan-language-enclave-Aragonese-Crown-1297 + second-largest-Mediterranean-island-24090km2 + second-Italian-autonomous-region-after-Sicily-1946 + Savoyard-Kingdom-of-Sardinia-1720-1861-+-pre-Italian-state + Editto-delle-Chiudende-1820-+-Sardinian-rural-revolts + Italian-Hague-1985-accession clusters within the corpus.
